title: Challenge Lab - Introduction to Amazon Lambda

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company wants to automatically copy files from one storage bucket to another when they’re uploaded, using Amazon Lambda to do it without a server. Your job is to make two buckets, set up permissions, create a Lambda function to copy files, add a trigger, and test it. Here’s how to do each step super simply:
Create two S3 Buckets: Create a source and destination bucket in us-east-1
Create a source and destination bucket in us-east-1
Create a Function in Lambada with Python run time and select pre-configured lambada-role: write python code to copy the files from 1 bucket to another
write python code to copy the files from 1 bucket to another
Adding Triggers to Lambda Function: ADD Trigger to Lambada function to trigger on adding file to source bucket
ADD Trigger to Lambada function to trigger on adding file to source bucket
Test the Lambda Function: Upload file in source bucket, it should automatically reflect in destination bucket
Upload file in source bucket, it should automatically reflect in destination bucket
Make sure everything is correct. In the lab console, click Validate to check if the buckets, Lambda function, IAM settings, and trigger are set up right.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the Lambda challenge!
